mission.title: wasmcloud: [RFC] Separate Actor and Provider SDKs
mission.description: [RFC] Separate Actor and Provider SDKs. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current wasmcloud deployment
steps.description:
Verify your wasmcloud version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n wasmcloud -l app.kubernetes.io/name=wasmcloud
helm list -n wasmcloud 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working wasmcloud installation.

steps.title: Review wasmcloud configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant wasmcloud configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n wasmcloud -l app.kubernetes.io/name=wasmcloud
kubectl get configmap -n wasmcloud -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=wasmcloud
```
This Request for Comment is soliciting feedback on the plan for separating the wasmCloud guest and provider SDKs.

steps.title: Apply the fix for [RFC] Separate Actor and Provider SDKs
steps.description:
This is the first step in addition of a "transitional" module support to `wasmcloud_actor` crate. When `module` feature is enabled, `wasmcloud_actor` relies on legacy wasmbus API. The module-based API mimics functionality exposed by component model-based support, meaning, that switch to components will be a breeze for users.

## Related Issues
## Release Information

## Consumer Impact

- All traits are now good-old `sync`, that may change in the future depeding on how the component model API

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n wasmcloud -l app.kubernetes.io/name=wasmcloud
kubectl get events -n wasmcloud --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "[RFC] Separate Actor and Provider SDKs" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: This is the first step in addition of a "transitional" module support to `wasmcloud_actor` crate. When `module` feature is enabled, `wasmcloud_actor` relies on legacy wasmbus API. The module-based API mimics functionality exposed by component model-based support, meaning, that switch to components will be a breeze for users.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with wasmcloud installed or the issue environment reproducible.
